project.name: PropManager System-Wide Optimization
project.description: Performance optimization for the entire PropManager system - controllers, services, models, API, and Vue components

qualityStandards.description: Production code for property management SaaS. Optimize for scalability, maintainability, and user experience.
principles: Database queries must be efficient - no N+1 queries
Use database-level aggregations over PHP-level processing
Cache expensive computations with appropriate TTL
API responses must be fast (<200ms for most endpoints)
Vue components should use computed properties for derived data

feedbackLoops.description: Run ALL feedback loops before committing. Do NOT commit if any fail.

commands.typecheck: npm run build -- --mode development 2>&1 | grep -i error

critical_n+1.description: Critical N+1 query patterns causing multiple queries per record

query_consolidation.description: Multiple separate queries that should be combined

database_aggregation.description: PHP-level processing that should be done in database

api_optimization.description: API endpoint optimizations - pagination, response size

items.title: Fix N+1 in DashboardService Super Admin Metrics
items.description: DashboardService.getSuperAdminMetrics() has severe N+1 issue: for each landlord, it runs 3 separate queries (units_count, occupied_units, monthly_revenue). With 10 landlords, this causes 30 extra queries.
steps: Refactor lines 48-59 to use withCount() for unit counts
Replace per-landlord getLandlordMonthlyRevenue() calls with single JOIN query
Refactor topLandlords (lines 61-70) to use ORDER BY with joined revenue calculation
Ensure the query returns all required data in single database round-trip
items.verification: Profile query count: should reduce from 30+ to 2-3 queries for 10 landlords

items.title: Consolidate FinanceStatsService Hub Stats Queries
items.description: getHubStats() runs 10+ separate count() queries that can be combined into a single query with CASE statements and multiple aggregations.
steps: Analyze lines 78-103 to identify all count/sum queries
Create single query using selectRaw() with CASE WHEN for conditional counts
Use GROUP BY and aggregate functions for counts by status
Test that results match original implementation

pattern.correct: selectRaw('SUM(CASE WHEN condition1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as count1, SUM(CASE WHEN condition2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as count2')

items.verification: Query count reduced from 10+ to 2-3 queries

items.title: Move Arrears Stats Calculation to Database
items.description: getArrearsStats() fetches ALL overdue invoices then calculates sum in PHP. This should use database-level SUM() to avoid loading potentially thousands of rows.
steps: Refactor lines 109-114 to use selectRaw() with SUM(total_due - amount_paid)
Add proper WHERE clauses for overdue status in the aggregation
Return scalar values instead of collection
Verify accuracy matches original implementation
items.verification: No longer loads full invoice collection for arrears calculation

items.title: Optimize Category Breakdown Query
items.description: Expense category breakdown loads full expenses with category relation, then groups in PHP. Should use GROUP BY with JOIN in database.
steps: Refactor lines 221-233 to use GROUP BY category_id with SUM()
JOIN categories table to get category names in same query
Return aggregated results directly instead of full collection
Handle null categories appropriately
items.verification: Query returns aggregated data, not full rows

items.title: Fix PaymentController Index N+1
items.description: Payment index query loads relationships but accessing payment.invoice.lease.tenant.email triggers N+1 at tenant level. Need to eager load full chain.
steps: Review lines 212-217 for relationship loading
Add 'invoice.lease.tenant' to with() clause
Verify all accessed relationships are eager loaded
Test with Laravel Debugbar to confirm query reduction
items.verification: Query count for 20 payments should not increase with more records

items.title: Consolidate PaymentController Stats Queries
items.description: Lines 265-277 run 3 separate raw queries for stats calculation that can be combined into single query with multiple aggregates.
steps: Combine the 3 selectRaw queries into single query
Use CASE statements for conditional aggregations
Return single result with all stats
Test accuracy of consolidated results

items.title: Optimize DashboardService Metrics Calculations
items.description: Lines 388-401 query same invoice subset twice for overdue_invoices count and overdue_amount sum. Should be single query.
steps: Combine count and sum into single selectRaw query
Use COUNT(*) and SUM() in same query
Apply same WHERE conditions once
Verify results match original implementation

items.title: Add Pagination to ReportController Arrears Endpoint
items.description: ReportController.arrears() returns all overdue invoices without pagination. For large datasets this causes slow responses and memory issues.
steps: Add pagination to lines 69-72 arrears query
Use cursor pagination for better performance on large datasets
Update API resource to include pagination metadata
Test with large dataset (1000+ invoices)
items.verification: Response includes pagination metadata and limits results per page

items.title: Move Arrears Aging Calculation to Database
items.description: Lines 83-95 categorize overdue invoices by age in PHP loop. This should use CASE statements in database for efficiency.
steps: Create database-level DATEDIFF and CASE categorization
Use GROUP BY on age categories with SUM()
Return pre-categorized results from database
Verify aging buckets match original logic (0-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+)

pattern.correct: selectRaw('CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(NOW(), due_date) <= 30 THEN "0-30" ... END as age_bucket, SUM(amount) as total')->groupBy('age_bucket')

items.verification: No PHP loop for aging categorization

items.title: Add Common Query Scopes to Invoice Model
items.description: Same invoice filters repeated throughout codebase. Add scopes for overdue, pending, outstanding, and paid statuses.
steps: Add scopeOverdue() - where status = 'overdue'
Add scopePending() - whereIn status ['sent', 'partial', 'overdue']
Add scopeOutstanding() - where amount_paid < total_due
Add scopePaid() - where status = 'paid'
Update existing code to use new scopes
items.verification: At least 5 occurrences updated to use new scopes

items.title: Add Active Scope to Lease Model
items.description: Active lease filter (where is_active = true) used in 20+ places throughout codebase. Add as model scope.
steps: Add scopeActive() to Lease model
Search codebase for ->where('is_active', true)
Update all occurrences to use ->active() scope
Verify no behavior changes

items.title: Consolidate Collection Rate Query
items.description: Lines 264-272 calculate collection rate from two separate queries (total invoiced, total collected). Should be single query.
steps: Combine queries using SUM with CASE statements
Calculate rate as (collected / invoiced) in database or single PHP operation
Handle division by zero case
Verify percentage calculation matches original

items.title: Replace Inline Filters with Computed Properties in Vue Components
items.description: Multiple components use inline collection filtering in v-for which re-runs on every render. Should use computed properties.
steps: Search for v-for with inline .filter() calls
Replace with computed properties that cache filtered results
Ensure computed dependencies are correctly defined
Test that filtered data updates when source changes
items.verification: No inline .filter() calls in v-for templates

items.title: Fix Redundant Relationship Loading in InvoiceController
items.description: InvoiceController loads relationships in query, then calls ->load() again with same relationships. Remove redundant loading.
steps: Review line 77 and line 116 for redundant load() calls
Remove load() if relationships already eager loaded in query
Verify relationships still available in views
Test invoice show and edit pages

items.title: DRY Finance Report Service Query Filters
items.description: Same filtering logic (landlord_id, date range, building_id) repeated 3 times for Invoice, Payment, and Expense queries. Extract into reusable method.
steps: Create private method for common query setup
Apply landlord_id, date range, and building_id filters in single place
Refactor Invoice, Payment, and Expense queries to use helper
Ensure all conditional filters work correctly
items.note: Deferred - queries use different date columns and conditions; abstraction would increase complexity without benefit

items.title: Cache Super Admin Metrics
items.description: DashboardService.getSuperAdminMetrics() runs expensive queries every request. Should cache results with short TTL.
steps: Add caching to getSuperAdminMetrics() with 5-minute TTL
Use cache tags for easy invalidation
Invalidate on significant events (new landlord, major payment)
Consider different TTL for different metrics
items.verification: Subsequent requests within TTL use cached data

items.title: Add Virtual Scrolling to Large List Components
items.description: Invoice, Payment, and Expense list components render full tables for 100+ rows. Implement virtual scrolling for better performance.
steps: Install vue-virtual-scroller or similar library
Apply to tables with potentially 100+ rows
Add CSS content-visibility for fallback
Test scroll performance with 500+ rows
items.verification: DOM node count stays constant regardless of row count

items.title: Fix Building Queries in PaymentController
items.description: Same buildings query repeated in multiple methods (lines 78-82, 289-292, 944-948). Extract to private method or cache.
steps: Create getBuildingsForDropdown() private method
Use in all places that need building list for forms
Consider caching building list if called multiple times per request
Ensure multi-tenant scoping is maintained

progressTracking.description: Track progress in progress.md file, committed to repo after each task

format.blockers: Any blockers or notes for next iteration

metrics: Query count per page < 15 for all pages
API response times < 200ms for common endpoints
No PHP-level aggregation of large datasets
Database indexes for all filtered/sorted columns
